compositionGate.notes: Overall two-column layout matches Figma across all 4 variants (light/dark x media left/right). Major elements are in correct positions, columns are properly reversed, dark mode card backgrounds render correctly, topic tag pills and buttons are present and styled. Image aspect ratio uses 4:3 per deliberate planning decision (DEC-009) rather than Figma's taller proportion.

issues.figmaValue: 32px (gap-5 between description paragraph and button wrapper)
issues.implementationValue:
0px (the

has not-last:mb-8 not-last:md:mb-12 but it is the LAST child of its parent .group div, so no margin applies)
issues.visualObservation: In the implementation screenshots, the button sits very close to the excerpt text with no visible gap. In the Figma design, there is a clear ~32px gap separating the body text from the LEARN MORE button.
issues.recommendation:
The .group wrapper div is breaking the not-last:mb-* pattern. The

is the only child of .group so not-last never matches. Fix by adding a margin-bottom to the .group div itself, or remove the .group wrapper and apply the margin directly to the

sibling relationship.

fix.verification: After fix, there should be a 32px gap (md: 48px) between the excerpt text and the button. Note: Figma shows 32px at all sizes, implementation uses 32px/48px at md breakpoint. The md:48px exceeds Figma by 16px but follows ContentWithMedia pattern.

issues.element: Gap between topic tag row and heading
issues.figmaValue: 24px (gap on Copy flex container between Eyebrow row and Content section)
issues.visualObservation: In the implementation, the topic tag pill appears closer to the heading text than in the Figma design. The Figma shows a comfortable 24px gap between the tag row and the heading, while the implementation shows a tighter 16px gap.
issues.recommendation: Change not-last:mb-4 (16px) to not-last:mb-6 (24px) on the topic tag row to match Figma's 24px gap.

fix.verification: After fix, the gap between the topic tag row and heading should be 24px, matching the Figma design.

issues.figmaValue: 48px (gap-6 on Content flex container between heading and text+button group)
issues.visualObservation: At desktop widths, the gap between the heading and the body text paragraph is visibly smaller in the implementation compared to the Figma. The Figma shows a generous 48px separation while the implementation shows approximately 32px.
issues.recommendation: Change not-last:mb-8 (32px) to not-last:mb-12 (48px) on the heading wrapper to match Figma's 48px gap. Alternatively, use not-last:mb-8 not-last:md:mb-12 for responsive scaling.

fix.verification: After fix, the gap between heading and description should be 48px on desktop (md+), 32px on mobile.

notes.observation: Figma uses line-height 1.1 for headings, but the theme's text-header-2 utility applies line-height 1.2. This is a theme-level design system choice, not a block-level bug. The 0.1 difference causes multiline headings to appear slightly taller. No action needed at block level.

Figma shows the image at approximately 624x677 (near 1:1.08 ratio). Implementation uses aspect-[4/3] which is 1:0.75. This was a deliberate planning decision (DEC-009) to match ContentWithMedia's single image layout for consistency. The visual difference is that the implementation image is wider and shorter than the Figma. Accepted per planning decision.

Figma dark mode cards have an extremely subtle texture overlay (noise image with mix-blend-color-dodge at 8% opacity). The implementation uses solid charcoal backgrounds without texture. The visual difference is negligible at normal viewing distance.

Figma root container uses items-center (both columns centered vertically). Implementation uses items-stretch (columns stretch to equal height) with flex items-center on the content column to vertically center the inner content. The visual result is equivalent -- content appears vertically centered relative to the image column. Acceptable implementation approach.

passes.description: Two-column grid layout matches Figma at desktop. Grid switches to single column on mobile. Column reversal via isReversed works correctly, placing content and media in the correct columns.
Dark mode card pseudo-elements render correctly with charcoal backgrounds and rounded-3xl corners on both content and media columns. Cards are properly spaced with -inset-8 (32px) padding on desktop.
Heading uses Anton font family at the correct size (text-header-2 = 64px on desktop, 48px on mobile). The font weight and style are correct (Regular, not italic).
Body text uses General Sans (font-sans) at 16px with 1.5 line-height and medium weight. Matches Figma design.
Topic tag pill text and eyebrow text use correct 14px uppercase medium weight styling with 1.1 line-height.
Topic tag pill uses accent-lighter background (#eeffb5) and accent border (#d4ff45), matching Figma's neon-green color tokens. The pill text is charcoal on green background.
Dark mode text colors switch to white correctly. Button switches to white background with charcoal text via .dark .btn-primary CSS.
Topic tag pill maintains its accent colors in dark mode (same green background/border with charcoal text), matching Figma's dark variant exactly.
Image rounded-3xl in light mode and rounded-xl in dark mode. Both match Figma values (large radius in light, 12px in dark).
Content is vertically centered within the content column via flex items-center. Button, heading, and text are all left-aligned.
All 8 test page variations render correctly: light/dark modes, reversed/default columns, custom button labels, small heading size (H3), and eyebrow text.
Mobile layout (375px) correctly stacks to single column with image above content by default. The row reversal option works as expected.
Button styling (btn-primary) matches Figma: rounded-full, charcoal fill in light mode, white fill in dark mode, uppercase text.

summary.overallAssessment: The FeaturedPost block's overall composition and visual structure match the Figma design well. All variations (light/dark, reversed columns, custom buttons, heading sizes, eyebrow text) work correctly. There are 3 major spacing issues: (1) no gap between description and button due to .group wrapper breaking the not-last margin pattern, (2) topic tag to heading gap is 16px instead of Figma's 24px, and (3) heading to description gap is 32px instead of Figma's 48px on desktop. These are all straightforward CSS spacing fixes.
